column1: review_text
betterthanI even thought
I love woodland scenics products, they are of consistently good quality and are pleasant to work with, easy to recommend for model railroad/scene builders.
This resin clay is fun to work with and adds all kinds of options to my mixed media art

I like these, super fun. Came on time, accurate order. Love that they dry quickly on more standard types of paper. I have not used them on other surfaces. Fun to combine with other materials, and I love how these are opaque, so if i mess up I can just paint over it again. Would recommend. I have these and the thinner ones... these fat ones are nice to cover up large areas quickly. And the Japanese writing is nice, although I can't read it. Just something to note.
Very good but packaging was awful!
Awesome...will buy again
Perfect for making bows-just what I wanted and expected.
Pretty colors and good quality paint.
I use the purple place as my no cut plate and it’s so much easier to keep track of now and I have a backup. They’re also very pretty.
I love this it’s my new crocheting bag to put my crocheting hooks and other stuff in.
Smooth writing

My old starter HF died and it was time to shop for the "real deal" type compressor for my airbrush's.
It arrived two days after I ordered it and was well packaged and ready to use in minutes.
The included bonus air hose was plug and play to the compressor and my brush quick disconnect system.
The Air Tank is a huge improvement to a stand alone compressor. No more pulsating and constant flow. Sometimes I need to lean on my airbrush and this sucker keeps up a the challenge.
This is not a toy, but the real deal.
Great for beginners. Very easy to use
They are difficult to separate and place where you need them. They are also thick which makes it a little cumbersome to sew. The jury is out on these for me, but I'll keep trying.
I love the ChiaoGoo needles! The cable is flexible from day one, and stays flexible over time. There are multiple sizes available, of which I have several.

The pictures and description of this product and price was misleading.  You are not told that 26 yards is the total length of 12 rolls  of elastic  and continue to mislead with images of 12 thick rolls.  What you actually send is 12 flat loops of elastic each measuring less than 2 yards,  Very misleading and not what I was expecting. You need to remeasure and reprice. Please beware customers.....
Fits my machine
It doesn't melt as a solid holographic finish, as I'd hoped.  What you get is a clear coating over little round pieces of colored glitter.  Disappointing.
Just as advertised
This yarn is so soft! I can't wait to get started on a blanket that I'm going to make my brother.
Can hold my small and large glue guns.
nice, flat, smooth, great

I purchased these blades in 2017 but had other blades at the time so I didn't get around to actually using these until this year.  I don't know if after a while they are no longer good or if they are just bad blades.  I have tried to use 4 of the 10 and end up throwing them away after a couple of times using because they skip.  I have now stopped using them.  The first time I used one I thought maybe it was just that blade but tried again a few months later to use one and it was the same thing but I kept trying to give them a chance.  Very disappointed
gift for 6 yr old.  she was good at keeping lines straight
Great storage system!  The only thing that it is missing is a handle.  Otherwise you just have to carry it horizontally.
Great variety of options, good price
I am blown away by the quality of this leather. It’s super soft but thick, flexible and just feels high quality. And the price is impressive for what you get! I highly recommend this!
I find it hard to gt the glue out when in the middle of a project, it seems to get stuck, then it's a it watery and takes a bit to hold. Not my favorite, but I will use it up since I paid for it.

These are cheaply made but still accomplish their task well. I sanded down the small beveler so it would be smooth rather than textured, the soft metal that these are made of actually made that process very easy and now I have two sizes of beveler.
The only complaint I have is that the texture on the backgrounder is not very sharp and leaves a soft rounded look.
Overall I'm very happy with this buy, I have all the main tools I want and a whole lot of extras
Perfect selection for amigurumi projects and stuffed animal restoration projects. The case it comes in is handy and keeps everything organized.

Great paints for craft work.
Not artist quality, but they said this.
They are what they were told.
For the price and correct application a great deal.

Make sure that your machine can use aftermarket needles before ordering because the spacing between may not fit. They don’t fit my older model metal machines but do fit my newer ones just fine. There are different sizes in the packet but I would sample-stitch cotton first before attempting anything heavier. For what they cost they’re ok.

I love these little things. I remember my grandmother used to use these to keep the dust ruffles on the beds neat and tidy. I’ve started to use the dust ruffles that are elastic. They stay in place with a large elastic band that fits snuggly around the bottom mattress. I was tired of using the regular dust ruffle and having to move the top mattress to straightened out that large piece of material that lay between the top and bottom mattress so thought the banded dust ruffle would work better. Well it does until someone catches the ruffle with their foot and ends up pulling it down. These nifty little things “screw” the dust ruffle in the correct place on the bottom mattress and now it doesn’t get stepped on or pulled down any more.
Our bed in the guest room is on raisers and now I can adjust the ruffle so it fits a little closer to the ground.
I originally thought that there would be more than enough for two beds but found by spacing all of them around the 3 sides of the bed there was less chance of ripping the material if someone still caught the ruffle.
They are a great find.
MIL loves it!
great item
Worked as expected. Needed for adding a little more room (ugh) to a costume jacket and worked perfectly wo being obvious. Strong quality hooks.
My son loves these. They dont roll which is a plus and easier to hold.
Seems to work fine. I've only used it twice. But worked as advertised both times.
A great tool for crafters and those who hand sew.

I used this to transfer a design onto a chalk board and it was so amazing! It transferred over flawlessly and I was able to use the same piece over and over. It was a little difficult to tell which side was dusted and which one wasn't but aside from that I was absolutely pleased with this product. I will buy it again for sure.
Good quality
Love these colors! Great for crochet wash clothes for kitchen and bath.
needed this

I've tried several times to use them & still go back to doing it by hand w/ an iron. I just haven't had any luck actually making these work for me. Frustrating to say the least. Operator error I'm sure but typically i can watch a couple of videos & figure things out. Not so much..LOL
I would recommend this product... Love it
This product was not user friendly nor sturdy when threading the sewing machine needle. Instructions were too vague. Had to YouTube the instructions.  Poor quality gadget for the money.
These are terrible but were a really low price, so you get what you pay for. I wouldn’t recommend them to anyone.
Very small. About 2x3. Not very moist. I was disappointed.
[[VIDEOID:5a3c4a6066286745af80a5ef6df6f618]] Nice and vibrant colors, easy to weed, perfect sheets size for my cameo 4, soft to touch and elegant finish.

Great price and awesome coverage
Perfect way to keep track of multiple pieces when quilting. Magnet closures are strong enough to hold multiple pieces tightly.

Beautiful colors and all 100 here. My only complaint is that they're not 14" as advertised aside from that great but for the money and will buy again for projects needing smaller than 14" zipper..

Update: the seams of the zippers are really cheap and easily come apart even without being pulled tight

My 9yr old craves stuff like this and really took to the whole set.  She is old enough to do it herself.  I think 6 would really be tough to do on their own.  I say 8 and up.  Great rainy day project.  With multiple people it is a one and done kit.  For one child, it has about 3 days worth of activities.

I have a Surebonder multi-temp gun that can be set from 220 degrees to 400 degrees, so am able to take full advantage of these glue sticks. They do indeed work over the full range of hot melt temps and the bond varies with temperature as well.

At the low temp end, I'm able to bond even styrofoam with no melting of the foam. At the high end, it's a good bond with metals, wood, etc.

Highly recommended.
Love it.  Finally a way to keep track of my rows.  Would recommend this counter.
These add a little sparkle between the larger beads.
love this ruler and all of the ones I have from creative grids.  They are easy to see through and they do not slip.  I just wish they made more sizes and shapes.

This serger stitches very nice, but it is very, very difficult to change needles and I do not recommend this to a beginner. I am not a beginner, but I encountered difficulty in changing the position of the needle from right to left or from left to right. It took me over an hour to get that needle in it!

these wooden disks aren't quite 5" around,  but they're fairly sturdy. Best glued together with clamps.

I brought these glitters so my friends and I can have a girl crafting date. Where we glitter-fying jars, glasses, vases, and wooden objects. As last minute gift fillers. These were the prefect size to add to the bundle of glitters we had been collecting and the girls and I were amazed by the openings on the side and the fact that we didn't have to open the whole jar every time we had to apply more glitter to smaller areas.

When we were finished I brought the rest of them home and add them to my nail art collection!

Dangerous. I bought these at a local craft store. They come with only one set of "interchangeable" handles. The handles are difficult to put on the blade and even harder to remove. The first time I tried to remove the handles, I stabbed myself in the hand with the end of the blade. I ended up using scrap clay to make permanent handles on each blade. Just bake the blades with the clay handles and you will be much safer.
The blades work well.
Use with my cricut explore air 2 and works perfectly in the vinyl setting.

Horrible! You know the old saying? You get what you pay for. This time it's true. I thought this will be just as good as Solvy and cheaper. Cheaper only! the paints in this kit come out opaque, bright, and smooth. its a pastel set which I thought was cute and I think its perfect for little DIY projects or beginner painters. even comes with brushes if you need.

I bought this bundle and I have laminated artwork and bound paper books. I am astounded at the price I paid as it was ridiculously low! As others have stated I too had a burning smell when I used the laminator for the first time. The second use the smell was noticeably absent. The books that came in this bundle I have not yet bound. I chose to make my own cover out of heavy card stock and hot glue and it worked exactly as all of the you tubers had said! Great resource! Next project is the hard photo books for Xmas presents. I recently used another brand for laminating sheets and the result was identical to the your story sheets. I will say the your story sheets are a more generous size and come in 12x12. The fact that one can laminate a scrapbook page that big is awesome. Happy crafting!
Quick ship and great sale!  Thanks so much!

I was surprised at how easy these are to use, I've used pain pens in the past and you had to put it in the oven and wait overnight. I was searching for those instructions for a while before realizing I didn't need to bake it. It is a little tricky to get the paint following, but once you do it writes easily. I wanted to use these to redo the measurements on my perfectly usable glass measuring cup. The medium point is a little big for what I wanted, but I think it'll be fine. After waiting moments for it to dry it was ready to go.

I just busted out my inks today after a really long time in my stash (I don’t stamp too often, and when I do it’s usually the Distress inks for techniques) to get started on my holiday cards - woohoo! - and they were still totally juicy from I purchased them three years ago. That’s kind of amazing.

I also panicked because the ink was beading on a new set of stamps, and thought that I was screwed. But then I walked away for an hour, came back and they had stamped absolutely perfectly. I had forgotten that Hero Arts ink takes a while to even out on the paper. I used Neenah 110lb, in case you’re curious.

I’m taking a star off only because there are so many color duplicates in these sets. I have so many Crimson, Pale Tomato and Soft Pools in my stash, it’s ridiculous. It would be great if each set had exclusive colors. Not that amazon can do anything about that....
I have just started machine embroidery, and this is a very nice selection of thread colors to use as a beginner witout having to invest a small fortune in threads!

This set is for the new Cricut Joy only. If you have one of the wider more expensive models, these pens won't fit without an adapter but Xinart has an [[ASIN:B08N5YFM9H equivalent pen set for your machine.]]
Good sampling of colors (33 different colors, there are 4 black pens), two ends a fine point and a medium point on each pen. They go for a long time (as long as you recap them after each use) and offer much more flexibility than the OEM pens with their two tips.
The set comes in a nice sturdy case, easy to put away.
A must have for the Cricut Joy -- but they will also make any kid very happy! Recommended.
These work perfectly  for the masks we made. And adjust to make the masks fit multiple sizes. We love them. Will buy them again! Arrived very quickly, too.

I ordered a 72 pack of colored pencils, and I primarily got into coloring to pass the time in the hotels when I have to travel; furthermore, lugging around the case the pencils came in wore down the packaging very quickly.

So I inevitably started looking for a pencil case that would be a bit sturdier during travel.

I picked this up, and I'm quite pleased with it. It definitely holds the pencils well. The elastic is really tight, and is a pretty tight fit to get the pencils in the first time. I have no doubt that over time the elastic will loosen up and make taking pencils out and putting them back in much quicker and easier.

There are basically 2 sections divided into a total of 4 "pages." Each section has its own zipper (so 2 zippers total), and each page has 6 elastic straps, which can hold 3 pencils.

It has held up well so far. If I encounter any durability issues, I'll come back and update, but I don't anticipate that happening. Would definitely recommend for anyone looking to make choosing colors easier, or for travel purposes.
Miyuki definitely makes the best seed beads. The color, shape, quality is consistently consistent.  I've ordered many from Amazon recently and am very pleased.

This product works great! It cuts fabric with ease & I luv the safety feature. I had a rotary cutter a few years ago and I accidentally cut off a small piece of of my finger. I ended up goung to the emergency room. Luckily the skin grew back, finger is goid as new! ;-)  Of course, I am way more careful now & the safety feature on THIS rotary cutter is great! I see myself using this tiol 4 quite a while.
Yep, difficult to use and get a clear medallion, but it is pretty.  This needs to be improved.

I love mine so I got a couple for Christmas gifts - they are so handy - easy to change out the blade when it gets dull. I've had mine for over 6 years and am still using the same blade.

I almost gave this 4 stars because it took awhile for delivery. But then I figured, what the heck, the item(s) arrived, and in good shape, and for free! Five stars it is! These boxes are great -- sturdy, stackable, just the right size for 6" paper, holds around 500 kami-weight paper. Transparent so you can see what's in there. The slots on the side allow you to flip thru your paper and select what you want without messing up the rest of the paper. The latch clips securely, so you can use the handle for transport without the box flipping open unexpectedly and disgorging all the contents. Very handy item, and inexpensive. Now, do they make the same item for 3" paper? That would be great -- hint-hint! Make it high enough to hold 1000 3" paper.

Maybe or maybe not. If you get over-filled bobbins (mine are not THAT full) then it would be no bargain. I cannot yet attest to the thread quality as I just received my package today... have not yet had a chance to use these; but if, as one other reviewer stated, the thread should be a low-quality product, then, again, this would not be a bargain. However, the bobbin class is correct for my sewing machines. That makes the purchase worth something. Even if I eventually wind up discarding the thread due to poor quality, I'll keep and use the bobbins. I did have a difficult time finding the end of the thread, had to unwind about a yard of thread from the first bobbin before I finally located the end.

This pack of 12 bobbins was the lowest priced package I could find on Amazon.com. I don't want or need 20 or 50 bobbins... but 3 or 4 would be too small a quantity. Since there's not a real fabric store in my town, I have to buy my sewing amenities where and when I find them.

Walmart doesn't count as a good source of sewing supplies; they carry small variety of things - including bobbins of this class, which only come in unfilled 3 packs, not larger sized packages...It is not a bargain to buy anything there, except perhaps a package or two of the pre-cut fabrics they offer, and maybe thread to match.

If Amazon allowed a 3.5 star rating, that's what I'd rate this purchase... it's better than 3 stars, but not really worth a full 4 stars, unless the thread is of decent quality.
Nice ink I love it
SOOOO MANYYY BEADDS!! Got these for my children’s class at church and I think I was more excited than them lol Beads are good for soooo many crafts! The assortment was phenomenal

I'm sure these boxes hold 12" x 12" paper, too, but each one of mine hold the fabric, pattern and notes for a future quilt. It works great to keep my materials and supplies organized. They stack nicely and simplify my sewing life.

I broke 4 needles before I learned how to center the hole correctly. On a Singer, you should have the stitch width at 0 to center it and you have to manually move the needle up and down to make certain that it does not rub the plates. Once done, you should be able to gather yards without mishap.

Ratio of cloth to ruffled results: ruffle @ every stitch is 1/2 of the original length at setting 1, 1/3 at setting 2 and 1/4 at 3. Haven't tried the 0, 4, and 8 yet. The tiny orange plastic wheel is where I change the settings. I measured 24" scraps before and after.

Amended 3/30/15: Ratchet the dial up for slippery fabric like organza. The material slides back so you lose some of the depth of the ruffle.

Hope this helps.

Kris Smith

I bought this machine over a year ago and have only started using it for the past month every day I am busy. The machine is very easy to use and has pictorial guides on how to thread both the needle and bobbins. I also find that the three speed setting on the machine are very helpful. I have used them all. The nice thing about the speed settings is that even if you "floor" the pedal it will not go any faster than the setting allows. This is helpful when sewing around tight corners or small items. I also made a sampler of all the stitches to see what this machine can do. The decorative stitches work best on the medium speed setting. Also I found stitch 11 requires both hands on the fabric and a little patience to have come out properly especially around curves.  Some of the downsides of this machine first you have to continually hold the reverse button to back tack I also do not like that it only takes their specific bobbins and they are plastic.  I also have had issues getting lint out of the machine. I have to unscrew the metal plate . In addition, when getting tangled thread out you have to be patient. The back of the manual says only untangle the thread . I had to go online and print out detailed directions on how to get it out of tight spaces and exactly what to check for . I also recommend you comb bind your manual so it lies flat and you can go to any page you want. I have called Brother customer service and they are very friendly and helpful. The only other issue I have is that the needles seem to break easily. I know some of the problem is human error but not three times in a month. Overall it does what it says but being computerized it is sensitive.
great

I love this little paper sizer. It's quite accurate and so light and easy. Perfect for when I'm traveling and buying jewelry to import for my online jewelry business.
It won't neccesarily take the place of a good quality sized made of a sturdy material like metal, but it works in a pinch.
The paper is laminated and does not read easily. I've had this sizer for a couple of years now and have travelled with it quite a few times with no issue. You do however have to be a bit mindful of how you pack it. While it is resistant to bends and cracks, it IS made of paper.
Arrived as promised.  Exactly what I ordered  Good value.

Was happy that I could get this quilting item and at the price that I got, and I use it constantly. it has been a great addition to my quilting.
Great for the different cuts that you have to move the material. It also makes the cuts easier for the cuts on the 10 inch layer cake and
they are more accurate. Thanks Again! Would recommend it to all quilters it's a great tool to have. Thanks Hope
I did not like the almost black darkness of this ink.
GET CHOICE
